bullet_points: Load pre-existing data from /home/node/work/projects/nbody_emulator/data/ic_final.npy and /home/node/work/projects/nbody_emulator/data/sim_metadata.npy.
Extract initial conditions and re-run the leapfrog integrator to generate 10 intermediate snapshots (t in {0.5, ..., 4.5}) for each of the 100 simulations.
Compute ground-truth accelerations for all particles at each snapshot.
Calculate the global Lyapunov time for each simulation by evolving perturbed initial conditions (delta r approx 10^-8) and measuring the divergence rate.
Print a summary of the Lyapunov time distribution (mean, median, min, max) and save a histogram plot of Lyapunov times to the database.
Implement a permutation-invariant Interaction Network (GNN) with edge functions processing relative positions and velocities.
Implement a modular switch to include the analytical gravitational force with softening epsilon=0.01 as a physical prior.
Enforce Newton’s Third Law by symmetrizing edge outputs.
Create and save fixed train/validation/test split indices (80/10/10) to the database to ensure consistency across all subsequent steps.
sub_tasks.sub_task: GNN Architecture Design and Data Partitioning

bullet_points: Train the GNN to minimize the MSE between predicted and ground-truth accelerations across the intermediate snapshots.
Use the Adam optimizer with a learning rate scheduler.
Save the training curve plot (training and validation loss vs. epoch) and print final training/validation MSE values.
Save the trained model weights.
Train two model variants: (A) Physics-Informed (with analytical force input) and (B) Learned-Only (raw kinematics only).
Evaluate both models on the test set to quantify the impact of the physical prior.
Produce a side-by-side bar chart comparing test-set acceleration MSE and print numerical MSE values for both models.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Ablation Study: Physics Prior vs. Learned Dynamics

bullet_points: Integrate the learned force field using both leapfrog (symplectic) and RK4 (non-symplectic) integrators.
Compute energy drift as the time-series variance of total energy normalized by the initial total energy.
Produce a plot of energy vs. time for four rollout conditions (GNN+leapfrog, GNN+RK4, MLP+leapfrog, MLP+RK4) and print all numerical drift values.
Generate test sets with N=25 and N=100 particles using the same Plummer sphere initialization parameters and fixed random seeds.
Evaluate the N=50 trained model on these new sets.
Print a quantitative table of per-particle MSE and energy conservation error for each N value compared to the N=50 baseline.
Compare the GNN emulator against a baseline MLP that maps t=0 directly to t=5.
Produce a single composite figure containing subplots for: (a) MSE vs. integration time, (b) energy drift comparison, and (c) trajectory divergence relative to the Lyapunov time.
Print all numerical benchmark values for inclusion in the final report.
Synthesize results from the ablation study, Lyapunov analysis, and N-scaling tests.

Write a structured Results section covering: (1) Lyapunov characterization, (2) acceleration learning and ablation findings, (3) Hamiltonian consistency and energy drift, (4) N-scaling generalization, and (5) GNN vs. MLP benchmark comparison.
Reference all plots generated in Steps 1–7 by their descriptive filenames.
